zh.lifeSummary: 當Dorothy Rose於1711年7月20日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德時，她的父親Jonathan Rose Sr.31歲，母親Abigail Hale23歲。她於1732年11月22日與Dr. Ebenezer Seward在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1746年7月1日在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普登格蘭維爾享年34歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普登格蘭維爾格蘭維爾 特麥 洛阿德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dorothy Rose于1711年7月20日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德时，她的父亲Jonathan Rose Sr.31岁，母亲Abigail Hale23岁。她于1732年11月22日与Dr. Ebenezer Seward在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1746年7月1日在美国马萨诸塞州哈普登格蘭維爾享年34岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州哈普登格蘭維爾格蘭維爾 特麥 洛阿德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosenbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans.
German: habitational name from any of numerous places so named (see   Rose   and   Bach  ).

variants.name: Roser
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew (see Rose 1), with the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant, or an occupational name for a rose grower, or a nickname for a lover of roses.
German (Röser): habitational name for someone from any of the places called Rös, Roes, or Rösa in Bavaria, Rhineland, and Saxony.
German (Röser): variant of Rösser (see Rosser 3).

variants.name: Roseman
meanings: English (Middlesex): from the Middle English female personal name Rosamunde (ancient Germanic Rosmund, from hros ‘horse’ + mund ‘protection’). The personal name was widely understood in the Middle Ages as Latin rosa munda ‘pure rose’ and therefore as an epithet of the Virgin Mary.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or name adopted by the husband of a woman bearing the Yiddish personal name Royze (see Rose 9).
Americanized form of German Rosemann or Rosmann (see Rossmann ).

variants.name: Roos
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Reisen
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): possibly an artificial name from northeastern Yiddish reyzn ‘roses’ (standard Yiddish royzn); see Rose 9.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Rosado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Carlos, Rafael, Angel, Manuel, Francisco, Ramon, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish: nickname for someone with a notably pink and white complexion, from Spanish rosado ‘pink’, Late Latin rosatus, a derivative of rosa (see Rose ).

variants.name: Roesel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto.
German (Rösel): diminutive of Rose .

variants.name: Rosette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Arturo, Cirilo, Consuelo, Cristina, Eloy, Eufrosina, Felipe, Jesus, Jorge, Manuel.

French: from a pet form of the female personal name   Rose   6 ‘rose’, or a habitational name from any of several places called (La) Rosette, e.g. in Yonne, denoting a place where roses grow. This surname is also found in the West Indies and Mauritius.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Roseth  .

variants.name: Rozsa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Sandor, Tibor, Zoltan, Zsuzsanna, Balint, Gabor, Gyula, Imre, Istvan, Katalin, Laszlo, Miklos.  German Armin, Erwin, Theodor.

Hungarian (Rózsa): from the female personal name Rózsa, from the vocabulary word rózsa ‘rose’. In some cases the surname might derive from an old male personal name of the same form. Compare Roja 2 and Rose 1.

variants.name: Roese
meanings: German (Röse):
variant of   Rose  .
possibly also a habitational name from a place called Rösa near Bitterfeld.

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

en.lifeSummary: When Dorothy Rose was born on 20 July 1711, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, her father, Jonathan Rose Sr., was 31 and her mother, Abigail Hale, was 23. She married Dr. Ebenezer Seward on 22 November 1732, in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 1 July 1746, in Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 34, and was buried in Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dorothy Rose est née le 20 juillet 1711, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Jonathan Rose Sr., avait 31 ans et sa mère, Abigail Hale, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Dr. Ebenezer Seward le 22 novembre 1732, à Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juillet 1746, à Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dorothy Rose am 20. Juli 1711 in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jonathan Rose Sr. 31 und ihre Mutter Abigail Hale 23. Sie hat am 22. November 1732 in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Dr. Ebenezer Seward geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Juli 1746 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dorothy Rose è nata il il 20 luglio 1711 nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, sua padre, Jonathan Rose Sr., aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Abigail Hale, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Dr. Ebenezer Seward il 22 novembre 1732, nel Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 1 luglio 1746, nel Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Dorothy Roseが1711年7月20日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで生まれた時，父Jonathan Rose Sr.は31歳，母Abigail Haleは23歳であった。1732年11月22日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベングイルフォードでDr. Ebenezer Sewardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1746年7月1日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ハンプデングランヴィルで34歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ハンプデングランヴィルグランヴィル センターメイン ロアド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Gr: 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 그랜빌 그랜빌 세터 맹 로드 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Dorothy Rose(이)가 1711년 7월 20일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jonathan Rose Sr.(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Abigail Hale(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1732년 11월 22일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 길퍼드에서 Dr. Ebenezer Seward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1746년 7월 1일 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 그랜빌에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 그랜빌 그랜빌 세터 맹 로드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dorothy Rose nasceu em 20 de julho de 1711, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, Jonathan Rose Sr., tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Abigail Hale, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Dr. Ebenezer Seward em 22 de novembro de 1732, em Guilford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de julho de 1746, em Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dorothy Rose родилась 20 июля 1711 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Jonathan Rose Sr. было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Abigail Hale, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Dr. Ebenezer Seward 22 ноября 1732 г. в местности гуилфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 1 июля 1746 г. в местности гранвилл, хампден, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности меин роад кеметири, гранвилл кентир, гранвилл, хампден, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dorothy Rose nació el 20 de julio de 1711, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Jonathan Rose Sr., tenía 31 años y su mamá, Abigail Hale, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Dr. Ebenezer Seward el 22 de noviembre de 1732, en Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 1 de julio de 1746, en Granville, Hampden, Masachusets, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultada en Main Road Cemetery, Granville Center, Granville, Hampden, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
